---
title: "Opus the Octopus — Knit Pattern"
author: "Sarah Cassie Burnett"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
    smooth-scroll: true
page-layout: article
date: today
---

> *Giant octopus made of superwash wool. Eight arms, infinite charm.*

![Octopus by Sarah](https://images4-a.ravelrycache.com/uploads/Mathbikerchick/860953936/9498EFB4-2D4B-4C83-BF3A-3D74E1DEBA46_medium2.jpeg?webp=0){fig-alt="Photo of knitted octopus" width="480"}

`$1000.00` *or*

[Buy the pattern →](https://www.ravelry.com/patterns/library/opus-the-octopus){.btn .btn-primary target="_blank"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Highlights

-   **Handmade**
-   Modular construction: body + 8 tentacles
-   Beginner-friendly shaping with clear charts
-   Printable PDF with photo walkthroughs

::: callout-note
## What makes it special?

The large floppy head is worked in the round; tentacles use simple increases to create a natural curl—fun, fast, and giftable.
:::

## Specs (Table)

| Feature | Value                                       |
|--------:|:--------------------------------------------|
|    Yarn | Worsted, superwash                          |
|   Gauge | 19 sts & 29 rows per 4 inches               |
| Needles | 3.75 mm (US 5)                              |
|    Size | 36 inches tall                              |
| Notions | Stitch markers, darning needle, safety eyes |

## Options (Task List)

-   [ ] Add safety eyes\
-   [x] 3D printed eyes (not child-safe)\
-   [x] Use two colors (contrast tentacles)

## Yarn choice: Malabrigo Arroyo

Marte

:   rich, variegated red-brown body

Moon Trio Full

:   lighter, speckled yarn

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## FAQ (Accordion)

::: panel-tabset
### Yarn Substitutions

Any DK yarn works held double. Acrylic also fine for easy care.

### Washing

Machine wash cold, gentle cycle. Reshape and dry flat.

### Safety

For babies/toddlers, skip safety eyes and embroider features.
:::

## Tiny Calculation (R Chunk)

```{r}
#| echo: true
# Estimate yarn cost for one octopus
skeins_needed <- 5
price_per_skein <- 16.50
stuffing <- 3.00
hours_knit <- 40 
hourly_rate <- 25.00
total_materials <- hours_knit * hourly_rate + skeins_needed * price_per_skein + stuffing
sprintf("$%.2f", total_materials)
```
